channel.title: bothSidesHeadlinesNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from bothSidesHeadlinesNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: New targeted therapy shows promise against aggressive childhood and adult cancers
item.description: UBC researchers develop drug that precisely seeks out and destroys cancer cells.

item.title: Long-acting HIV shots appeal to many but uptake remains low
item.description: Patients prefer occasional injection over daily pill, but adoption lags.

item.title: Human cell model recreates Alzheimer's-linked tau pathology and synapse loss
item.description: LMU researchers develop model with potential for novel neurodegenerative therapies.

item.title: Global fertility reversal unfolding, may upend who becomes parent
item.description: Birth rates falling worldwide; fertility rate describes average children per woman.

item.title: Most Americans worried about brain health but few know how to protect it
item.description: Alzheimer's Association survey finds brain health ranks as important as physical health.

item.title: RHR: The GLP-1 blind spot: what Ozempic won't do for metabolic health
item.description: Podcast explores science behind GLP-1 medications beyond appetite and weight loss.

item.title: RHR: Shaking up the salt myth
item.description: Decades of warnings on sodium reduction for blood pressure are challenged.

item.title: RHR: Mouth taping beyond the hype: what science really says
item.description: Chris Kresser explores viral trend of sleeping with mouth taped.

item.title: RHR: Erythritol: the 'safe' sweetener that's anything but
item.description: Popular sugar-free sweetener may have hidden health risks.

item.title: RHR: Preserving cognitive function as you age, with Tommy Wood
item.description: Neuroscientist and physician discuss strategies for brain health.
